verses.text: Daniel dreams of four winds striving on the sea, and four great beasts coming up, each unlike the rest. Turmoil of nations. Out of the world's chaos God brings forth His purpose.
Daniel dreams of four winds striving on the sea, and four great beasts coming up, each unlike the rest. Turmoil of nations. Out of the world's chaos God brings forth His purpose.
Daniel dreams of four winds striving on the sea, and four great beasts coming up, each unlike the rest. Turmoil of nations. Out of the world's chaos God brings forth His purpose.
The first like a lion with eagle's wings, the second like a bear, the third like a four-headed leopard. Empires in symbol. Each kingdom rises with its own marks of power.
The first like a lion with eagle's wings, the second like a bear, the third like a four-headed leopard. Empires in symbol. Each kingdom rises with its own marks of power.
The first like a lion with eagle's wings, the second like a bear, the third like a four-headed leopard. Empires in symbol. Each kingdom rises with its own marks of power.
The fourth is dreadful, with iron teeth and ten horns, and a little horn rises 'speaking great things.' Fierce arrogance. The last power blasphemes against heaven.
The fourth is dreadful, with iron teeth and ten horns, and a little horn rises 'speaking great things.' Fierce arrogance. The last power blasphemes against heaven.
The thrones are set, the Ancient of days sits, and 'the judgment was set, and the books were opened.' The court of heaven. Above all earthly powers, God judges all.
The thrones are set, the Ancient of days sits, and 'the judgment was set, and the books were opened.' The court of heaven. Above all earthly powers, God judges all.
The beast is slain for the horn's great words, and the rest lose their dominion. Pride answered. The blasphemer's boasting hastens his destruction.
The beast is slain for the horn's great words, and the rest lose their dominion. Pride answered. The blasphemer's boasting hastens his destruction.
'One like the Son of man' comes to the Ancient of days and receives an everlasting dominion that shall not pass away. Christ enthroned. His kingdom alone is eternal.
'One like the Son of man' comes to the Ancient of days and receives an everlasting dominion that shall not pass away. Christ enthroned. His kingdom alone is eternal.
Daniel, grieved, is told the four beasts are four kings, but 'the saints... shall take the kingdom' for ever. Hope for the godly. The world's powers fade; the saints inherit all.
Daniel, grieved, is told the four beasts are four kings, but 'the saints... shall take the kingdom' for ever. Hope for the godly. The world's powers fade; the saints inherit all.
Daniel, grieved, is told the four beasts are four kings, but 'the saints... shall take the kingdom' for ever. Hope for the godly. The world's powers fade; the saints inherit all.
Daniel, grieved, is told the four beasts are four kings, but 'the saints... shall take the kingdom' for ever. Hope for the godly. The world's powers fade; the saints inherit all.
He asks of the fourth beast and the horn that warred with the saints, 'until the Ancient of days came' and judgment was given to them. Suffering then victory. The saints endure, then reign.
He asks of the fourth beast and the horn that warred with the saints, 'until the Ancient of days came' and judgment was given to them. Suffering then victory. The saints endure, then reign.
He asks of the fourth beast and the horn that warred with the saints, 'until the Ancient of days came' and judgment was given to them. Suffering then victory. The saints endure, then reign.
He asks of the fourth beast and the horn that warred with the saints, 'until the Ancient of days came' and judgment was given to them. Suffering then victory. The saints endure, then reign.

The fourth kingdom and its horn shall speak against the most High and 'wear out the saints,' for 'a time and times and the dividing of time.' Limited oppression. Persecution has its bounds, fixed by God.

The fourth kingdom and its horn shall speak against the most High and 'wear out the saints,' for 'a time and times and the dividing of time.' Limited oppression. Persecution has its bounds, fixed by God.

The fourth kingdom and its horn shall speak against the most High and 'wear out the saints,' for 'a time and times and the dividing of time.' Limited oppression. Persecution has its bounds, fixed by God.
But the judgment shall sit, his dominion taken, and the kingdom given to the saints, 'an everlasting kingdom.' The end is sure. However long the trial, the saints' triumph is certain.
